result:
This is fine to keep — it's Next's standard auto-generated config, harmless and normal to have in a TypeScript Next.js project. Everything is verified and working. Here's the summary:

## What was wrong

The site is mid-pivot from an old product ("InboxZap," email cleanup) to a new one ("Lumina," AI meeting assistant), and the rebrand left real bugs actively suppressing organic traffic:

1. **Every page's `<title>`/meta description still said "InboxZap"** while the content was 100% Lumina — wrong snippet in every search result and social share.
2. **`/pricing` and `/features` 404'd** — including the homepage's own "Start free" CTA target.
3. **The FAQ nav link redirected to a `/#faq` anchor that didn't exist** on the page.
4. **The blog was invisible to Google** — client-side fetch to a non-existent `/api/posts`, so crawlers saw an empty shell.
5. **`sitemap.ts` had a literal `TODO` excluding all blog posts**, and there was no route to serve individual posts at all.
6. Per your GSC export, the old InboxZap posts (`post-1`...`post-12`) still carry real impressions (`post-1`: 3,900) but now 404 — moot either way since their topics (bulk email delete, Gmail storage) are irrelevant to the new product.

## What I fixed

- Corrected title/description/OG metadata to describe Lumina.
- Built real `/pricing` and `/features` pages (content grounded in the existing homepage copy).
- Added an actual `id="faq"` FAQ section to the homepage (also good for AI-answer-engine snippet capture).
- Rebuilt the blog as server-rendered with a working `/blog/[slug]` route and a real sitemap entry per post.
- Wrote and published two launch articles targeting keywords that actually match the product: *"How to Automate Meeting Notes with AI"* and *"How to Choose an AI Meeting Assistant for Your Team."*
- Verified all of this with a real `npm install && npm run build && next start` — every route returns 200, metadata is correct per-page, sitemap lists all real URLs. (I couldn't verify against the `http://127.0.0.1:62112` URL you gave me — it accepted TCP connections but never returned an HTTP response even after 2 minutes, across multiple attempts. That's worth checking on your end.)

## Left for you

- No `DISTRIBB_API_KEY` is set, so I couldn't hit Distribb's real keyword-research, backlink-exchange, or GSC endpoints — worth connecting once you're ready to scale content and get real backlinks.
- `/img/og-lumina.png` is referenced but doesn't exist — needs an actual brand asset.
- `npm install` flagged `next@14.2.5` has a known security vulnerability with a patch available — unrelated to this task but worth a look.
- Next step once traffic starts coming in for the *new* keywords: connect GSC for real and start the optimize/audit loop the skill describes.
